name: Projectile Reflection
description: A defensive or reactive action that redirects an incoming projectile back, away, or toward a new target.
design_notes: Projectile reflection is satisfying when eligibility, timing, and new ownership are clear. It should define reflectable projectile types, timing windows, new direction, damage ownership, and whether reflected shots can be reflected again.
design_purpose: Turn ranged pressure into a timing or positioning opportunity.
Reward players for reading projectile speed and attack direction.
Create counterplay against enemies or players who rely on ranged attacks.
player_fantasy: The player feels skillful and untouchable when enemy fire becomes their weapon.

parameters.name: reflection_active_window_ms
parameters.description: Window in which incoming projectiles can be reflected.

parameters.name: reflected_velocity_multiplier
parameters.description: Velocity multiplier applied to the projectile after reflection ownership changes.

parameters.name: reflected_damage_multiplier
parameters.description: Damage multiplier applied to reflected projectiles.

parameters.name: reflection_angle_tolerance_degrees
parameters.description: Allowed incoming angle relative to defender facing.

edge_cases: Explosive, homing, beam, hitscan, and area projectiles need explicit reflect eligibility.
Ownership transfer must prevent reflected shots from damaging the reflector's allies unless intended.
Reflecting a projectile on the same frame it expires or hits another target needs ordering rules.
Infinite reflection loops need bounce limits or state changes.
common_bugs: Projectile changes direction visually but damage owner remains the original attacker.
Reflected projectile immediately collides with the reflector because it spawns inside their collider.
Homing projectiles keep tracking the player after reflection.
Reflection uses client timing and fails under latency even when feedback shows success.
balancing_notes: Wide reflection windows can invalidate ranged enemies unless limited by stamina, cooldown, angle, or projectile tags.
Reflect damage multipliers should be tuned against enemy health and projectile frequency.
accessibility_notes: Use readable projectile silhouettes and reflect cues when timing is required.
Offer alternate defenses if reflection is mandatory and depends on very narrow timing.

implementation_notes.unity: During reflection_active_window_ms, validate projectile_id, reflectable tags, incoming angle, and owner_id. Transfer ownership, update velocity, and move the projectile outside the reflector collider before resuming simulation.
implementation_notes.godot: Check projectile Area collisions against reflector state and angle tolerance. On success, rewrite owner_id, velocity, damage multiplier, and homing target before emitting reflect feedback.
implementation_notes.unreal: Handle reflection on the server by validating projectile class or tags, guard angle, and timing window. Update replicated projectile owner, instigator, velocity, and bounce count atomically.
implementation_notes.web: Resolve reflection as a projectile transaction keyed by projectile_id and reflector_id. Change owner, direction, speed, damage, and reflection count in one step to avoid loops or friendly-fire errors.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
example_games.note: Uses projectile reflection in boss and enemy encounters.

example_games.title: Overwatch
example_games.note: Uses Genji's Deflect to redirect incoming projectiles.
